sections.paragraph:
βέβαιος, α, ον [develops from the pf. of βαίνω ‘walk, step’: βέβηϰα ‘stand’, hence ‘firm, steady’] in NT always used metaphorically and with focus on stability linked with continuity

—a. secure of hope in imagery of an anchor Hb 6:19.

—b. as simple metaphor: firmly based, firm 2 Cor 1:7 of hope that is not in danger of disruption; Hb 3:6 v.l., 14; dependable 2 Pt 1:19; valid Ro 4:16; Hb 2:2; 9:17; 2 Pt 1:10.
